title: × Laeliocattleya caloglossa
intro: × Laeliocattleya caloglossa, commonly known as Caloglossa, is a hybrid orchid species derived from the natural hybridization of Laelia and Cattleya genera. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Caloglossa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Caloglossa include tea infusions and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: × Laeliocattleya caloglossa is a hybrid orchid, classified within the genus Laeliocattleya, which belongs to the subtribe Laeliinae, a group of intergeneric hybrids between Cattleya and Laelia orchids. This classification reflects its complex and deliberate breeding, combining characteristics from both parent genera. As a hybrid orchid, × Laeliocattleya caloglossa's classification is not strictly within a single species but rather a unique combination of genetic traits. The Laeliocattleya genus is further divided into several intergeneric hybrids, each with its distinct characteristics. The specific classification of × Laeliocattleya caloglossa is a result of ongoing orchid breeding and classification efforts.
